steps.title: Check current Perses version
steps.description:
Verify your Perses installation supports dashboard tags. This feature was introduced via PR #2683.
```bash
# If running via Docker
docker exec perses perses --version

# If running on Kubernetes
kubectl get pods -n perses -o jsonpath='{.items[0].spec.containers[0].image}'
```
Upgrade if you are running an older version that does not support tags.

steps.title: Upgrade Perses to a version with dashboard tags
steps.description:
Upgrade your Perses deployment to include the dashboard tags feature:
```bash
# If running via Docker
docker pull persesdev/perses:latest
docker stop perses && docker rm perses
docker run -d --name perses -p 8080:8080 -v perses-data:/etc/perses persesdev/perses:latest

# If running on Kubernetes via Helm
helm repo update
helm upgrade perses perses/perses -n perses
```

steps.title: Add tags to dashboards
steps.description:
Dashboard tags allow you to categorize and search dashboards more conveniently. Add tags to your dashboard metadata:
```yaml
Kind: Dashboard
metadata:
  name: my-dashboard
  tags:
    - oncall
    - high_severity
    - production
```
Tags can also be managed through the Perses UI when editing a dashboard. Use the tags field in the dashboard settings to add, remove, or modify tags.

steps.title: Verify dashboard tags work
steps.description:
Confirm the dashboard tags feature is working:
1. Open the Perses UI and navigate to the dashboard list
2. Verify that dashboards display their assigned tags
3. Use the tag-based search or filter to find dashboards by tag
4. Edit a dashboard and confirm you can add or remove tags from the UI

resolution.summary: Added a 'tags' field to the Dashboard metadata struct, allowing users to assign string tags to dashboards for categorization and search. Tags are stored as a list in the dashboard metadata and are surfaced in the UI for filtering.
codeSnippets:
Kind: Dashboard
metadata:
  name: my-dashboard
  tags:
    - oncall
    - high_severity
    - production

prerequisites.description: A running Perses instance (via Docker or Kubernetes) with at least one dashboard configured.
